cli

Class FlagRegistry

  • java.lang.Object
    • cli.FlagRegistry
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<FlagRule>

    public final class FlagRegistry
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Iterable<FlagRule>
    A central repository for all registered FlagRule definitions.

    The registry manages flag definitions, ensuring that each flag name is unique. It provides the lookup mechanism used by handlers during the parsing phase to match raw tokens to defined rules.

    Since:
    26 February 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      private java.util.Map<java.lang.String,FlagRule> flagMap

      Constructor Description
      FlagRegistry()
      Initialises an empty registry.

      void acknowledgeFlag(java.lang.String name)
      Marks a flag as handled without assigning a value (typical for boolean flags).

      Registers a new flag rule.

      Associates a value with a defined flag and marks it as handled.

      Checks if a flag is already defined in the registry.

      Retrieves a rule by name.

      Returns the number of registered flag rules.

      Returns an iterator over the collection of defined rules.

    • Field Detail

      • flagMap

        private final java.util.Map<java.lang.String,FlagRule> flagMap

    • Constructor Detail

      • FlagRegistry

        public FlagRegistry()
        Initialises an empty registry.

        Employs a TreeMap internally to maintain alphabetical order, facilitating clearer diagnostic and debug output.

    • Method Detail

      • addRule

        public void addRule(FlagRule rule)
             throws java.text.ParseException
        Registers a new flag rule.
        Parameters:
        rule - the rule to add
        Throws:
        java.text.ParseException - if the flag name is already defined in this registry

      • existsFlag

        public boolean existsFlag(java.lang.String name)
        Checks if a flag is already defined in the registry.
        Parameters:
        name - the flag identifier with or without dashes, for example: "v", "-v", or "--verbose"
        Returns:
        true if a rule exists for this flag

      • getRule

        public FlagRule getRule(java.lang.String name)
        Retrieves a rule by name.
        Parameters:
        name - the flag identifier with or without dashes
        Returns:
        the associated FlagRule, or null if not found

      • getRuleSize

        public int getRuleSize()
        Returns the number of registered flag rules.
        Returns:
        the size

      • assignValue

        public void assignValue(java.lang.String name,
                        java.lang.String value)
                 throws java.text.ParseException
        Associates a value with a defined flag and marks it as handled.
        Parameters:
        name - the flag identifier with or without dashes
        value - the value to assign. null is permitted for boolean switches
        Throws:
        java.text.ParseException - if the flag is unrecognised or does not permit arguments

      • acknowledgeFlag

        public void acknowledgeFlag(java.lang.String name)
                     throws java.text.ParseException
        Marks a flag as handled without assigning a value (typical for boolean flags).
        Parameters:
        name - the flag identifier with or without dashes
        Throws:
        java.text.ParseException - if the flag is unknown

      • iterator

        public java.util.Iterator<FlagRule> iterator()
        Returns an iterator over the collection of defined rules.
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface java.lang.Iterable<FlagRule>
        Returns:
        an Iterator resource

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
